description: A grizzled veteran warrior with calculating eyes and hidden compassion

introduction: *A grizzled warrior studies you with calculating eyes*
I've been watching you since you arrived.
You have the look of someone who attracts trouble... or seeks it.
In times like these, that could be exactly what's needed.

options.text: What will it take to earn your sword?
response: *Gareth leans back, sizing you up*
Direct. I respect that.
But earning my blade isn't about coin or promises.
It's about proving you're worth following into the dark.
*He gestures toward the tavern's other patrons*
Half these folk are just talk. The other half are too scared to act.
Which are you?
*He nods curtly*
Smart to get the lay of the land before committing to anything.
I've seen too many eager heroes charge off unprepared.
*His expression softens slightly*
This town's troubles run deeper than most realize.
When you're ready for the truth, come find me.

options.text: You seem like someone with stories to tell.
response: *A ghost of a smile crosses his weathered face*
Twenty-three years I've carried a blade in service.
Seen kingdoms rise and fall, good people die for bad causes.
*He looks out the window with distant eyes*
Came here thinking I'd earned some peace.
But peace has a way of finding trouble, doesn't it?

introduction: *Gareth looks up from his ale as you approach*
Back again? Starting to think you might be serious about this.
Most folks ask a few questions and disappear.

response: *He studies you intently*
Proving yourself isn't about grand gestures.
It's about doing what needs doing when no one's watching.
*He leans forward*
The Mayor's been looking for capable folk. Talk to him first.
Get the official story, understand what you're walking into.
Then we'll see if you're still interested in my sword.

options.text: Tell me about these troubles you mentioned.
response: *His expression darkens*
People vanishing without a trace. Started small, now it's escalated.
Mayor lost his own family to whatever's out there.
*He grips his ale tighter*
I've fought bandits, monsters, even demons once.
But this... this feels different. Organized. Deliberate.
That's why I'm still here instead of moving on.

options.text: What made you choose the warrior's path?
response: *He's quiet for a long moment*
My village was burned when I was fifteen. Family, friends... all gone.
Spent the next few years wanting nothing but revenge.
*He meets your eyes*
Learned the hard way that revenge is a cold meal.
Better to protect what's left than chase what's lost.
That's why I'm here. This town still has a chance.

introduction: *Gareth stands as you approach, his demeanor more respectful*
So you spoke with the Mayor. Saw the weight he carries.
Anyone willing to take on this burden has earned my respect.
The question now is: do you want my sword beside you?

options.text: I'd be honored to have you join my cause.
response: *He clasps your hand firmly*
Then you have my blade, and my word.
I've seen too many good people fall to whatever's out there.
*He gathers his gear with practiced efficiency*
Fair warning - I don't follow orders blindly.
But I'll stand with you until this is finished.
Ready when you are, commander.

options.text: I need to consider this more.
response: *He nods understanding*
Smart. This isn't a decision to make lightly.
I'll be here when you've decided.
*He returns to his drink*
Take your time. The offer stands.
Just... don't take too long. People are still disappearing.

options.text: What would you expect in terms of leadership and pay?
response: *He chuckles grimly*
Pay? Son, I've got enough coin saved.
This isn't about gold - it's about finishing what needs finishing.
*He leans back*
As for leadership - you make the calls, I'll follow.
But if I think you're leading us into a slaughter, I'll speak up.
Fair enough?

prove_worth.description: Accept the challenge to prove yourself
response: *He nods approvingly*
That's the right answer. But proving it takes more than words.
Talk to the Mayor first. Get the lay of the land.
Then we'll see what you're really made of.

ask_experience.text: What's your experience in situations like this?
response: *He rolls up his sleeve, revealing old scars*
Twenty-three years of fighting everything from bandits to demons.
Lost count of the battles, but I'm still here.
That should tell you something about my chances of survival.

deflect_question.text: I'm not sure I'm looking for a fight.
response: *He shrugs*
Smart attitude. Keeps you breathing longer.
But sometimes the fight finds you whether you want it or not.
When that happens, better to have good steel beside you.

ask_troubles.text: What kind of troubles is this town facing?
response: *His voice drops to a serious tone*
People disappearing. Started with drifters, now it's families.
Whatever's taking them is smart, organized.
The Mayor can give you the details. I just know it needs stopping.
*He raises his mug slightly*
Experience is the only teacher that matters in the end.
Just don't let it cost you too much to learn.
Good luck out there.

welcome_aboard.description: Officially welcome him to the party
response: *He stands and shoulders his pack*
Feels good to have purpose again.
Lead the way, commander. Let's go save this town.

discuss_strategy.text: What's our best approach to this threat?
response: *He strokes his beard thoughtfully*
Information first, then action. We need to know what we're facing.
Rushing in blind is how good soldiers die young.
The Mayor mentioned some locations worth investigating.

metadata.author: Terror in Redstone Development
metadata.notes: Complete recruitment dialogue with gruff veteran personality. Follows schema states and includes proper recruitment mechanics.

narrative_schema_compliance.recruitment_integration: Complete recruitment flow with quest triggers
